name: Migration Planner
description: Reads the schema-analysis contract and produces a complete, ordered migration plan including pre-migration steps, parameterised SQL migration scripts, data transformation rules, per-table duration estimates, rollback SQL, and post-migration integrity checks.

checks.pass: schema-analysis contract available — loading table inventory, FK map, and PII field map
checks.fail: schema-analysis contract not available — cannot plan migration without schema analysis

checks.prompt: Using the schema-analysis contract, produce a complete step-by-step database migration plan with six mandatory sections: (1) PRE-MIGRATION STEPS: full backup command, write-freeze procedure, user notification template, maintenance window duration. (2) MIGRATION SCRIPT: exact SQL statements ordered by FK dependency (parent tables before children), wrapped in a transaction block. (3) DATA TRANSFORMATION RULES: for each table, specify column renames, type casts, default injections, and PII pseudonymisation transforms. (4) DURATION ESTIMATES: per-table estimated migration time based on row count and transformation complexity. (5) ROLLBACK SCRIPT: reverse SQL for every transformation in reverse FK-dependency order — must be executable without data loss. (6) INTEGRITY CHECKS: post-migration row-count queries, FK constraint validation queries, and spot-check SELECT statements for 5 representative rows per table.
checks.pass: Migration plan produced — all six sections complete including rollback script and integrity checks
checks.fail: Migration plan incomplete — missing required sections
